_comment: RAT storage/v1 conformance vectors — ADR-003 shared golden data. The SINGLE source of truth for the storage axis. Both independent references (Go: examples/storage/inmemory-go, Python: examples/storage/inmemory-py) load THIS file and drive StorageService.VendCredentials over real gRPC. Storage owns byte storage + credential vending; the control plane never sees bytes, it only asks storage to vend short-TTL, prefix-scoped, TENANT-scoped credentials (the C7 enforcement point — reviews/01 Finding 3, reviews/04). The credential blob is opaque/provider-specific in production (an STS token set); for conformance the references encode the GRANTED SCOPE as a JSON 'scope receipt' {tenant, prefix, mode, expires_unix_ms} so the harness can assert the security obligation: the vended creds are bound to the caller's tenant (read from the rat-callmeta-bin metadata header, ADR-007 — NOT a request field, so it can't be forged) + the requested prefix + mode. This axis is the first reference that CONSUMES identity from the metadata envelope. NOTE: `prefix` is a LOGICAL, provider-neutral path (scheme-less) so every storage backend can resolve it per its own scheme — the in-memory refs echo it into the scope receipt; the round-2 local-fs ref resolves it to a real filesystem path under the tenant root (and enforces containment). The scope receipt MAY carry extra provider-specific fields (e.g. resolved_path) that these vectors ignore.
